title: Main checkout uses reserved main identity
context: The main checkout of a git repository is a special case: it exists independently of linked worktrees and must have a stable, predictable identity for isolation to work correctly across all worktrees in a repository.
decision: The main checkout uses the reserved Worktree ID 'main' and Worktree Label 'main'.
alternatives: Treat the main checkout as having no identity and special-case it throughout the implementation.
Derive the main checkout's identity the same way as linked worktrees, with no reserved value.
rationale: This keeps the main checkout explicit and avoids special casing through absence of identity. The main checkout has valid, predictable identity rather than being treated as a special case or an unmanaged worktree.
consequences: The reserved identity 'main' must not be assignable to a linked worktree.
Implementation must detect and refuse attempts to assign 'main' to a non-main checkout.
The main checkout participates fully in the identity model rather than being an edge case.
reversal_trigger: If the reserved 'main' identity creates irreconcilable conflicts with a repository naming convention, a configurable reserved identity may be considered post-1.0.
